danger_signals.visual_form: Junkbots and Junkman character sprites in environment
danger_signals.meaning: Contact with enemies causes limb loss; progressive damage state

danger_signals.visual_form: Level obstacles and traps (visual design not detailed in sources)
danger_signals.meaning: Contact with stage hazards causes limb loss, equivalent threat to enemies

danger_signals.visual_form: Slick's body parts become detached and fall away
danger_signals.meaning: Damage state reached; visual indicator of health deterioration

danger_signals.visual_form: Slick's head falls off after all limbs lost
danger_signals.meaning: Imminent death; life loss triggered after this state

safety_signals.meaning: Restores lost limbs; recovery from damage state
Player agency and primary safe interaction point

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Rescue mission objective: freeing Dr. Zub from captivity

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: One Junkbot serves as midlevel boss per area; Junkman as final boss
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Five distinct areas each containing Junkbot territory; Junkman's domain represents final confrontation space

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Slick intact with all limbs; standard level navigation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Slick responds to player input normally; can move, jump, interact with environment

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: One or more limbs detached from Slick's body; limb still visible in environment
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Slick mobility or appearance changed; reduced capability or visual impairment

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Only Slick's torso and head remain; headless body state imminent
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: One additional hit triggers head detachment and life loss

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Slick's head separates from body completely
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Life counter decrements; level reset or game over state initiated

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Screwdriver collected; limbs reattach to Slick
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Slick regains full mobility and returns to normal exploration state

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Large enemy sprite; distinct arena or encounter space within area
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Combat engagement; escalated threat level; different enemy attack patterns

environmental_storytelling.example: Five distinct areas serve as stages in rescue mission structure; each area contains one Junkbot guardian, creating hierarchical threat escalation toward final boss
Dr. Zub established as captive/rescue target held by Junkman; player mission framed as liberation narrative
Junkbots occupy distinct level areas; final domain controlled by Junkman; spatial layout reflects power structure
Limb loss system visualizes vulnerability of crash dummy bodies; screwdriver recovery system reflects repair/maintenance theme inherent to dummy character concept

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Full movement capability; responsive to player input; can execute platforming actions
Reduced functionality; visual impairment; continued vulnerability to enemy/hazard contact
Extreme vulnerability; one additional hit triggers immediate death; no recovery without screwdriver
Mobile enemy threat; collision with Slick inflicts limb loss; patrol or engage behavior not detailed
Enhanced threat level; serves as climactic obstacle; defeat required to complete rescue mission
Passive pickup; collection triggers limb reattachment and health recovery

notes: Sources provided limited detail on visual manifestation systems. Game uses toy line IP (Crash Dummies action figures) as thematic foundation, influencing dismemberment-based damage model. SNES version specifically uses platformer progression with five-area structure and dual-boss-type hierarchy. Narrative context adapted from 1993 FOX Kids animated TV special suggests light-hearted tone despite damage system. T3 data tier reflects qualitative description rather than empirical measurement; exact visual asset descriptions, color palettes, and sprite designs not documented in available sources.
sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - The Incredible Crash Dummies (SNES)
Wikipedia - The Incredible Crash Dummies (video game)
